title: CLAUDE.md
text: file provides guidance Claude Code (claude.ai/code) working code repository.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: nlmixr2est provides core nonlinear mixed-effects (NLME) estimation routines nlmixr2 ecosystem. implements multiple estimation methods PK/PD modeling: SAEM, FOCEI, FOCE, FO/FOI, Laplace, AGQ, NLM-family methods (nlminb, nlm, bobyqa, newuoa, n1qn1, lbfgsb3c, optim, uobyqa, nls). depends heavily rxode2 ODE solving – models compiled rxode2 C code runtime.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: package requires compilation (NeedsCompilation: yes). C++17 required (set src/Makevars). changing C++ files, devtools::load_all() recompiles.
code: # Load package for interactive development devtools::load_all()  # Run all tests devtools::test()  # Run a single test file testthat::test_file("tests/testthat/test-focei-1.R")  # Build documentation (regenerates NAMESPACE via roxygen2) devtools::document()  # Full R CMD check devtools::check()  # Set the number of threads for parallel processing (e.g., SAEM) rxode2::setRxThreads(threads = 4)  # Use 4 threads

title: CLAUDE.md
text: Entry point nlmixr2() R/nlmixr2.R, calls nlmixr2Est() – S3 generic dispatching est argument string. estimation method R file (R/saem.R, R/focei.R, R/fo.R, R/foce.R, R/laplace.R, R/agq.R, R/nlm.R, R/nlminb.R, R/bobyqa.R, etc.) plus corresponding control function (e.g., saemControl(), foceiControl()).

title: CLAUDE.md
text: estimation, model UI objects translated rxode2-compatible representations via rxPipeline() (R/rxPipeline.R) method-specific *RxUiGet handlers (R/saemRxUiGet.R, R/saemRxUiGetModel.R, R/splitModelRxUiGet.R, R/nlmeRxUiGet.R). ini block sets initial estimates; mu-referencing (population parameters appear linearly ETAs) detected handled specially SAEM efficiency.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: R/hook.R provides hook system pre-processing steps run estimation: bounded parameter transforms (R/preProcessBoundedTransform.R), covariate handling (R/preProcessCovariatesPresent.R), data/UI validation (R/preProcessDataUi.R), zero-omega fixing (R/preProcessZeroOmega.R), literal fix processing (R/preProcessLiteralFix.R).

title: CLAUDE.md
text: Core numerical work C++17 RcppArmadillo/RcppEigen: - saem.cpp – SAEM algorithm kernel - nlm.cpp / innerPoint.cpp – NLM-family optimization - cwres.cpp, npde.cpp, ires.cpp – residual calculations - censResid.cpp, censEst.cpp – M2/M3/M4 censoring methods - nearPD.cpp – nearest positive-definite matrix - uninformativeEtas.cpp, shrink.cpp – ETA diagnostics

what: Registering C/C++ (.Call) routines – src/init.c is MANUAL
title: CLAUDE.md
text: src/init.c hand-maintains R_registerRoutines callMethods[] table (Rcpp’s generated R_init_nlmixr2est RcppExports.cpp used – init.c owns R_init_nlmixr2est). .Call entry lists explicit prototype HARDCODED argument count, e.g. {"_nlmixr2est_foo", (DL_FUNC) &_nlmixr2est_foo, 5}. add [[Rcpp::export]] function change existing one’s argument count, MUST : 1. Rcpp::compileAttributes(".") – regenerates src/RcppExports.cpp + R/RcppExports.R; 2. hand-edit src/init.c – add/update forward SEXP ...(SEXP, ...) prototype callMethods[] entry’s arg count. Skipping step 2 gives runtime .Call error like Incorrect number arguments (N), expecting M '_nlmixr2est_foo' (loaded registration table still old arity), compile error – survives clean rebuild. header change still needs rm -f src/*.o src/*.rebuilding.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: Fit results nlmixr2FitData objects (data frame subclass). Post-fit accessors use nmObjGet S3 dispatch (R/nmObjGet.R, R/nmObjHandle.R). Residuals added lazily via addCwres() addNpde().

title: CLAUDE.md
text: tests/testthat/helper-zzz-fits.R pre-fits models caches individual test files can reference fitted objects without re-running estimation. Fixture .rds files live tests/testthat/fixtures/.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: tests/testthat.R keeps CI CRAN oversubscribing core-limited runners (historically caused 6h timeouts / exit-143 “runner received shutdown signal”): testthat workers: single worker CI (CI=true) CRAN; everywhere else testthat manages Config/testthat/parallel (parallel: true, edition: 3 DESCRIPTION) normally. rxode2 / data.table within-solve threads: capped 2 CRAN ; CI locally rxode2 manages threads. pass non-parallel reporter test_check() – testthat silently falls back serial reporter$capabilities$parallel_support FALSE (e.g. LocationReporter), defeats parallel config.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: mu2 referencing (R/mu2.R) detects validates mu-referenced parameters; violations produce warnings, errors, SAEM IOV (inter-occasion variability) support R/iov.R special handling mu-referencing hooks sharedControl() function (R/sharedControl.R) defines options common across estimation methods R/nlmixr2Est.R contains central S3 dispatch table – look understand methods exist

title: CLAUDE.md
text: Avoid AI-typical verbosity: multi-paragraph explanations code comments, worked examples numeric anecdotes embedded comments, restating code obviously . One line usually enough; explain non-obvious . Roxygen descriptions short (sentence two) – keep every @param/@return tag, don’t pad @details tutorial-style prose. NEWS.md entries terse, single-bullet, past-tense, similar existing entries older version headers (e.g. # nlmixr2est 6.0.1 / 6.0.0) – multi-line narrations root cause mechanism. Unicode source, comments, docs (em dashes, smart quotes, arrows, etc.) – CRAN policy expects plain ASCII. Use --, '/", -> instead.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: R Style: variable function names R must camelCase (e.g., simData, fitSaem, oneCompartmentMix). use snake_case dot.case R objects. C++ Style: Follow Rcpp/Armadillo conventions. Use snake_case camelCase appropriate.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: emitting rxode2 model text programmatically (e.g. augmented sensitivity models R/foceiCovAnalytic.R / R/foceiGradAnalytic.R, inner/pred models R/focei.R): Name every generated output variable rx_<name>_ (leading rx_, trailing _), matching inner model’s rx_pred_ / rx__sens_..._ convention. Two reasons: (1) user can define model variable plain name (e.g. rvar, predf, f1), un-prefixed generated name can collide silently corrupt model; (2) rxode2 parses top-level x = <constant> initial value (x becomes parameter, dropping solve columns) UNLESS name rx_<name>_ form – keep constant assignment real lhs output column. constant sensitivity (e.g. d(R)/d(eta)=0 additive error) comes back column constant, special-casing needed. Use rx_predf_, rx_f1_<dir>, rx_rvarf_, etc. – never predf, f1_..., rvar, rxPredF (plain rxFoo without trailing _ still parsed init). Use = variable read back (output column solve). Use ~ (suppressed assignment) intermediate compute output – extra = output columns shift positional column layout add solve overhead. Declare theta/eta inputs model covariates (ui$allCovs) param(...) solve parameter order fixed positional (values keyed name, pinning order keeps input layout stable across builds). Covariates supplied event data solve time must still declared param(...).

title: Authors and Citation
text: Fidler M, Xiong Y, Schoemaker R, Wilkins J, Trame M, Hooijmaijers R, Post T, Wang W (2026). nlmixr: Nonlinear Mixed Effects Models Population Pharmacokinetics Pharmacodynamics. R package version 6.1.0, https://CRAN.R-project.org/package=nlmixr. Fidler M, Wilkins J, Hooijmaijers R, Post T, Schoemaker R, Trame M, Xiong Y, Wang W (2019). “Nonlinear Mixed-Effects Model Development Simulation Using nlmixr Related R Open-Source Packages.” CPT: Pharmacometrics & Systems Pharmacology, 8(9), 621–633. https://doi.org/10.1002/psp4.12445. Schoemaker R, Fidler M, Laveille C, Wilkins J, Hooijmaijers R, Post T, Trame M, Xiong Y, Wang W (2019). “Performance SAEM FOCEI Algorithms Open-Source, Nonlinear Mixed Effect Modeling Tool nlmixr.” CPT: Pharmacometrics & Systems Pharmacology, 8(12), 923–930. https://doi.org/10.1002/psp4.12471.
code: @Manual{,   title = {{nlmixr}: Nonlinear Mixed Effects Models in Population Pharmacokinetics and Pharmacodynamics},   author = {Matthew Fidler and Yuan Xiong and Rik Schoemaker and Justin Wilkins and Mirjam Trame and Richard Hooijmaijers and Teun Post and Wenping Wang},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 6.1.0},   url = {https://CRAN.R-project.org/package=nlmixr}, } @Article{,   title = {Nonlinear Mixed-Effects Model Development and Simulation Using nlmixr and Related R Open-Source Packages},   author = {Matthew Fidler and Justin Wilkins and Richard Hooijmaijers and Teun Post and Rik Schoemaker and Mirjam Trame and Yuan Xiong and Wenping Wang},   journal = {CPT: Pharmacometrics \& Systems Pharmacology},   year = {2019},   volume = {8},   pages = {621--633},   number = {9},   month = {sep},   abstract = {nlmixr is a free and open-source R package for fitting nonlinear pharmacokinetic (PK), pharmacodynamic (PD), joint PK-PD, and quantitative systems pharmacology mixed-effects models. Currently, nlmixr is capable of fitting both traditional compartmental PK models as well as more complex models implemented using ordinary differential equations. We believe that, over time, it will become a capable, credible alternative to commercial software tools, such as NONMEM, Monolix, and Phoenix NLME.},   address = {Hoboken},   publisher = {John Wiley and Sons Inc.},   url = {https://doi.org/10.1002/psp4.12445}, } @Article{,   title = {Performance of the SAEM and FOCEI Algorithms in the Open-Source, Nonlinear Mixed Effect Modeling Tool nlmixr},   author = {Rik Schoemaker and Matthew Fidler and Christian Laveille and Justin Wilkins and Richard Hooijmaijers and Teun Post and Mirjam Trame and Yuan Xiong and Wenping Wang},   journal = {CPT: Pharmacometrics \& Systems Pharmacology},   year = {2019},   volume = {8},   pages = {923--930},   number = {12},   month = {dec},   abstract = {The free and open-source package nlmixr implements pharmacometric nonlinear mixed effects model parameter estimation in R. It provides a uniform language to define pharmacometric models using ordinary differential equations. Performances of the stochastic approximation expectation-maximization (SAEM) and first order-conditional estimation with interaction (FOCEI) algorithms in nlmixr were compared with those found in the industry standards, Monolix and NONMEM, using the following two scenarios: a simple model fit to 500 sparsely sampled data sets and a range of more complex compartmental models with linear and nonlinear clearance fit to data sets with rich sampling. Estimation results obtained from nlmixr for FOCEI and SAEM matched the corresponding output from NONMEM/FOCEI and Monolix/SAEM closely both in terms of parameter estimates and associated standard errors. These results indicate that nlmixr may provide a viable alternative to existing tools for pharmacometric parameter estimation.},   url = {https://doi.org/10.1002/psp4.12471}, }

title: Copilot instructions for nlmixr2est
text: Purpose - Provide concise guidance future Copilot sessions repository (R package C++ sources). Build, test, lint commands - Load package (compiles C/C++): devtools::load_all() - Run full test suite: devtools::test() - Run single test file (example): testthat::test_file(“tests/testthat/test-focei-1.R”) - Build documentation (regenerates NAMESPACE): devtools::document() - Full R CMD check: devtools::check() shell: R CMD check . - Note: package NeedsCompilation: yes requires C++17 (see src/Makevars). devtools::load_all() usual dev workflow recompile native code. High-level architecture (big picture) - Entry points: R/nlmixr2.R -> nlmixr2Est() (S3 dispatch). See R/nlmixr2Est.R dispatch table. - Estimation methods: method R file control function (e.g., R/saem.R + R/saemControl.R, R/focei.R + R/foceiControl.R). - UI → rxode2 pipeline: model UI objects translated rxode2 via R/rxPipeline.R method-specific RxUiGet handlers (R/saemRxUiGet.R, R/splitModelRxUiGet.R, R/nlmeRxUiGet.R). - Pre-processing hooks: R/hook.R registers pre-processing steps. Implementations live R/preProcess.R (bounded transforms, covariates, data/ui validation, zero-omega handling, literal fix processing). - Core numerical layer: C++ src/ (saem.cpp, nlm.cpp, inner.cpp, cwres.cpp, nearPD.cpp, etc.). expose R interfaces via Rcpp exports (RcppExports.cpp / R/RcppExports.R). - Post-fit accessors: Fits nlmixr2FitData objects; accessors lazy computations implemented R/nmObjGet*.R addCwres()/addNpde(). - Tests fixtures: helper-zzz-fits.R pre-fits models stores .rds fixtures tests/testthat/fixtures avoid re-running long estimations. Key conventions repository-specific patterns - “mu2” (mu-referencing): Special handling detection R/mu2.R. SAEM optimizations depend mu-referencing; validate via mu2 code tests. - Preprocess hooks: Use R/hook.R add/remove pre-processing logic. Search preProcess* files see available hooks. - Control objects: sharedControl() centralizes options common estimators; method-specific control functions delegate . - Native code workflow: C++ changes require recompilation via devtools::load_all() (C++17, see src/Makevars). Pay attention Rcpp Armadillo/Eigen usage. - Tests: Many tests rely cached fixtures (tests/testthat/fixtures). adding tests require long fits, add fixtures avoid CI slowness. - Naming: Estimation methods map 1:1 R/*.R files (saem.R, focei.R, fo.R, nlm.R, etc.). Look understand behavior control options. Files read first (high signal) - CLAUDE.md (repo) — high-level notes architecture commands - README.md / README.Rmd — package overview - R/nlmixr2Est.R — S3 dispatch method registry - R/rxPipeline.R R/saemRxUiGet.R — UI → rxode2 translation - R/hook.R R/preProcess.R — pre-processing hook system - src/saem.cpp, src/nlm.cpp, src/inner.cpp — core numeric kernels - tests/testthat/helper-zzz-fits.R tests/testthat/fixtures/ — test fixtures expectations AI assistant configs - CLAUDE.md present — include guidance reasoning architecture commands. - .claude/settings.local.json exists (local CLAUDE settings); assistant rule files detected. Quick tips Copilot sessions - Prefer reading: R/nlmixr2Est.R, R/rxPipeline.R, R/hook.R proposing estimator changes. - change touching src/, ensure compilation via devtools::load_all() run smallest test covers change. - Avoid re-running long SAEM fits CI; use tests/fixtures helper-zzz-fits.R introducing heavy tests. Contact - uncertain design decision, point R/sharedControl.R method-specific control files precedent.

code: # install.packages("remotes") remotes::install_github("nlmixr2/rxode2") remotes::install_github("nlmixr2/nlmixr2data") remotes::install_github("nlmixr2/lotri") remotes::install_github("nlmixr2/nlmixr2est") library(nlmixr2est) #> Loading required package: nlmixr2data  ## The basic model consists of an ini block that has initial estimates one.compartment <- function() {   ini({     tka <- 0.45; label("Ka")     tcl <- 1; label("Cl")     tv <- 3.45; label("V")     eta.ka ~ 0.6     eta.cl ~ 0.3     eta.v ~ 0.1     add.sd <- 0.7   })   # and a model block with the error specification and model specification   model({     ka <- exp(tka + eta.ka)     cl <- exp(tcl + eta.cl)     v <- exp(tv + eta.v)     d/dt(depot) = -ka * depot     d/dt(center) = ka * depot - cl / v * center     cp = center / v     cp ~ add(add.sd)   }) }  ## The fit is performed by the function nlmixr/nlmixr2 specifying the model, data and estimate fit <- nlmixr2(one.compartment, theo_sd,  est="saem", saemControl(print=0)) #> ℹ parameter labels from comments are typically ignored in non-interactive mode #> ℹ Need to run with the source intact to parse comments #> → loading into symengine environment... #> → pruning branches (`if`/`else`) of saem model... #> ✔ done #> → finding duplicate expressions in saem model... #> [====|====|====|====|====|====|====|====|====|====] 0:00:00 #> → optimizing duplicate expressions in saem model... #> [====|====|====|====|====|====|====|====|====|====] 0:00:00 #> ✔ done #> → creating rxode2 include directory #> → getting R compile options #> → precompiling headers #> ✔ done #> using C compiler: 'gcc.exe (GCC) 14.2.0' #> ℹ calculate uninformed etas #> ℹ done #> rxode2 3.0.4.9000 using 8 threads (see ?getRxThreads) #>  #> Attaching package: 'rxode2' #> The following objects are masked from 'package:nlmixr2est': #>  #>     boxCox, yeoJohnson #> Calculating covariance matrix #> → loading into symengine environment... #> → pruning branches (`if`/`else`) of saem model... #> ✔ done #> → finding duplicate expressions in saem predOnly model 0... #> → finding duplicate expressions in saem predOnly model 1... #> → optimizing duplicate expressions in saem predOnly model 1... #> → finding duplicate expressions in saem predOnly model 2... #> ✔ done #> using C compiler: 'gcc.exe (GCC) 14.2.0' #> → Calculating residuals/tables #> ✔ done #> → compress origData in nlmixr2 object, save 5952 #> → compress phiM in nlmixr2 object, save 63504 #> → compress parHistData in nlmixr2 object, save 13928 #> → compress code: # \donttest{  one.cmt <- function() {   ini({     ## You may label each parameter with a comment     tka <- 0.45 # Log Ka     tcl <- log(c(0, 2.7, 100)) # Log Cl     ## This works with interactive models     ## You may also label the preceding line with label("label text")     tv <- 3.45; label("log V")     ## the label("Label name") works with all models     eta.ka ~ 0.6     eta.cl ~ 0.3     eta.v ~ 0.1     add.sd <- 0.7   })   model({     ka <- exp(tka + eta.ka)     cl <- exp(tcl + eta.cl)     v <- exp(tv + eta.v)     linCmt() ~ add(add.sd)   }) }  f <- nlmixr2(one.cmt, theo_sd, "saem") #>   #>   #>   #>   #> ℹ parameter labels from comments are typically ignored in non-interactive mode #> ℹ Need to run with the source intact to parse comments #>   #>   #> → loading into symengine environment... #> → pruning branches (`if`/`else`) of saem model... #> ✔ done #> → finding duplicate expressions in saem model... #> ✔ done #> ℹ calculate uninformed etas #> ℹ done #> Calculating covariance matrix #> → loading into symengine environment... #> → pruning branches (`if`/`else`) of saem model... #> ✔ done #> → finding duplicate expressions in saem predOnly model 0... #> → finding duplicate expressions in saem predOnly model 1... #> → finding duplicate expressions in saem predOnly model 2... #> → optimizing duplicate expressions in saem predOnly model 2... #> ✔ done #>   #>   #> → Calculating residuals/tables #> ✔ done #> → compress origData in nlmixr2 object, save 6584 #> → compress parHistData in nlmixr2 object, save 8968 #> → compress phiM in nlmixr2 object, save 448192  # even though you may have forgotten to add the NPDE, you can add it to the data.frame:  f <- addNpde(f) #> → Add NPDE #>   #>   #> ✔ done  # }

what: Control options for the agq estimation method — agqControl
title: Control options for the agq estimation method — agqControl
text: control options adaptive Gauss-Hermite quadrature likelihood.  Note nAGQ=1 Laplace method.

title: Control options for the agq estimation method — agqControl
code: agqControl(   sigdig = 3,   nAGQ = 2,   ...,   interaction = TRUE,   agqLow = -Inf,   agqHi = Inf )

title: Control options for the agq estimation method — agqControl
text: sigdig Optimization significant digits; controls inner/outer optimization tolerance (10^-sigdig), ODE solver tolerance (0.5*10^(-sigdig-2), 0.5*10^(-sigdig-1.5) sensitivity/steady-state liblsoda), boundary check tolerance (5*10^(-sigdig+1)). nAGQ Number Gauss-Hermite adaptive quadrature points. `0` disables AGQ; `1` equivalent Laplace. Cost grows quickly ETAs: EBE found, expect `nAGQ^neta` (even `nAGQ`) `(nAGQ^neta)-1` (odd `nAGQ`) additional evaluations per subject. ... Parameters used default `foceiControl()` interaction boolean, Interaction term model, case default `TRUE`; adaptive quadrature, normal distribution Hessian calculated foce() approximation agqLow lower bound adaptive quadrature log-likelihood. default -Inf; original nlmixr's gnlmm -700. agqHi upper bound adaptive quadrature log-likelihood.  default Inf; original nlmixr's gnlmm 400.

analytic-covariance scope; using the finite-difference covariance instead #> → Calculating residuals/tables #> ✔ done   p <- pump  p$dv <- p$y p$time <- 0 # dummy time  f <- function() {   ini({     t1 <- 1     t2 <- 1     t3 <- 1     t4 <- 1     eta1 ~ 1   })   model({     if (group == 1) {        lp <- t1 + t2 * logtstd     } else {        lp <- t3 + t4 * logtstd     }     lp <- lp + eta1     lam <- exp(lp)     y ~ dpois(lam)   }) }  fit <- nlmixr(f, p, est="agq", control=agqControl(nAGQ=5)) #>   #>   #>   #>   #> ℹ parameter labels from comments are typically ignored in non-interactive mode #> ℹ Need to run with the source intact to parse comments #> → loading into symengine environment... #> → pruning branches (`if`/`else`) of full model... #> ✔ done #> → calculate ∂(f)/∂(η) #> → finding duplicate expressions in inner model... #> → optimizing duplicate expressions in inner model... #> → finding duplicate expressions in EBE model... #> → optimizing duplicate expressions in EBE model... #> → compiling inner model... #>   #>   #> ✔ done #> → finding duplicate expressions in FD model... #> → optimizing duplicate expressions in FD model... #> → compiling EBE model... #>   #>   #> ✔ done #> → compiling events FD model... #>   #>   #> ✔ done #> covType="analytic": adaptive Gaussian quadrature (nAGQ > 1) is out of analytic-covariance scope; using the finite-difference covariance instead #> → Calculating residuals/tables #> ✔ done  one.cmt <- function() {   ini({     ## You may label each parameter with a comment     tka <- 0.45 # Log Ka     tcl <- log(c(0, 2.7, 100)) # Log Cl     ## This works with interactive models     ## You may also label the preceding line with label("label text")     tv <- 3.45; label("log V")     ## the label("Label name") works with all models     eta.ka ~ 0.6     eta.cl ~ 0.3     eta.v ~ 0.1     add.sd <- 0.7  })  model({    ka <- exp(tka + eta.ka)    cl <- exp(tcl + eta.cl)    v <- exp(tv + eta.v)    linCmt() ~ add(add.sd)  }) }  fit <- nlmixr(one.cmt, theo_sd, est="agq") #>   #>   #>   #>   #> ℹ parameter labels from comments are typically ignored in non-interactive mode #> ℹ Need to run with the source intact to parse comments #> → loading into symengine environment... #> → pruning branches (`if`/`else`) of full model... #> ✔ done #> → calculate ∂(f)/∂(η) #> → calculate ∂(R²)/∂(η) #> → finding duplicate expressions in inner model... #> → optimizing duplicate expressions in inner model... #> → finding duplicate expressions in EBE model... #> → optimizing duplicate expressions in EBE model... #> → compiling inner model... #>   #>   #> ✔ done #> → finding duplicate expressions in FD model... #> → compiling EBE model... #>   #>   #> ✔ done #> → compiling events FD model... #>   #>   #> ✔ done #> covType="analytic": a linCmt() model is out of analytic-covariance scope; using the finite-difference covariance instead #> → Calculating residuals/tables #> ✔ done  # }

code: bobyqaControl(   npt = NULL,   rhobeg = NULL,   rhoend = NULL,   iprint = 0L,   maxfun = 100000L,   returnBobyqa = FALSE,   stickyRecalcN = 4,   maxOdeRecalc = 5,   odeRecalcFactor = 10^(0.5),   indTolRelax = TRUE,   useColor = NULL,   printNcol = NULL,   print = 1L,   normType = c("rescale2", "mean", "rescale", "std", "len", "constant"),   scaleType = c("nlmixr2", "norm", "mult", "multAdd"),   scaleCmax = 1e+05,   scaleCmin = 1e-05,   scaleC = NULL,   scaleTo = 1,   rxControl = NULL,   optExpression = TRUE,   sumProd = FALSE,   literalFix = TRUE,   literalFixRes = TRUE,   addProp = c("combined2", "combined1"),   calcTables = TRUE,   compress = FALSE,   covMethod = c("r", ""),   adjObf = TRUE,   ci = 0.95,   sigdig = 4,   sigdigTable = NULL,   eventSens = c("jump", "fd"),   ... )

title: Control for bobyqa estimation method in nlmixr2 — bobyqaControl
text: npt Number points quadratic approximation objective; must `[n+2, (n+1)(n+2)/2]`. Defaults `min(n*2, n+2)`. rhobeg Initial trust region radius (`rhoend`, must satisfy `0 < rhoend < rhobeg`). Defaults `min(0.95, 0.2*max(abs(par)))`; adjusted upward smaller `abs(upper-lower)/2`. rhoend Final trust region radius. Defaults `1e-6*rhobeg`. iprint Controls amount printing (`0`=none, `1`=start/end , `2`=new rho, `3`=every function evaluation, `>3`=every `iprint` evaluations). Default `0`. maxfun maximum allowed number function evaluations. exceeded, method terminate. returnBobyqa return bobyqa output instead nlmixr2 fit stickyRecalcN number bad ODE solves reducing atol/rtol rest problem. maxOdeRecalc Maximum number times reduce ODE tolerances try resolve system bad ODE solve. odeRecalcFactor ODE recalculation factor ODE solving goes bad, factor rtol/atol reduced indTolRelax `TRUE` (default), subjects whose ODE solve produced NaN/Inf tolerances relaxed, relaxed tolerance persists across optimizer calls (sticky). `FALSE`, subjects tolerances relaxed retry tolerances reset afterward. useColor Logical (`NULL`) emit ANSI bold/color escapes iteration print. `NULL` (default) defers [crayon::has_color()]. printNcol Integer (`NULL`) parameter columns per row wrapping. `NULL` (default) uses `floor((getOption("width") - 23) / 12)`. print Either scalar print-frequency (`0` = suppress, `1` (default) = every evaluation, `N` = every Nth), pre-built [iterPrintControl()] object. Equivalent `iterPrintControl(every = print, ncol = printNcol, useColor = useColor)`. normType Parameter normalization/scaling used get scaled initial values scaleType, form Vscaled = (Vunscaled-C1)/C2 (see Feature Scaling; rescale2 follows OptdesX manual): "rescale2" scales parameters (-1, 1); "rescale" (min-max) scales (0, 1); "mean" centers mean range (0, 1); "std" standardizes mean/sd; "len" scales unit (Euclidean) length; "constant" performs normalization (C1=0, C2=1). scaleType scaling scheme nlmixr2: "nlmixr2" (default) scales (current-init)*scaleC[] + scaleTo, scaleTo normType scales scaleC; "norm" uses simple scaling normType; "mult" scales multiplicatively current/init*scaleTo; "multAdd" scales linearly ((current-init)+scaleTo) parameters exponential block (e.g. exp(theta)) multiplicatively otherwise. scaleCmax Maximum value scaleC prevent overflow. scaleCmin Minimum value scaleC prevent underflow. scaleC Scaling constant used scaleType="nlmixr2"; specified, chosen parameter type keep gradient sizes similar log scale: `1` exp()-transformed/power/boxCox/ yeoJohnson parameters, `0.5*abs(est)` additive/proportional/ lognormal error parameters, `abs(1/digamma(est+1))` factorials, `log(abs(est))*abs(est)` log-scale parameters. May set explicitly per parameter defaults apply well. scaleTo Scale initial parameter estimate value. default 1.  zero , scaling performed. rxControl `rxode2` ODE solving options fitting, created `rxControl()` optExpression Optimize rxode2 expression speed calculation. default turned . sumProd boolean indicating model change multiplication high precision multiplication sums high precision sums using PreciseSums package.  default FALSE. literalFix boolean, substitute fixed population values literals re-adjust ui parameter estimates optimization; Default `TRUE`. literalFixRes boolean, substitute fixed population values literals re-adjust ui parameter estimates optimization; Default `TRUE`. addProp Type additive-plus-proportional error: `"combined1"`, standard deviations add: $$y = f + (+ b\times f^c) \times \varepsilon$$; `"combined2"`, variances add: $$y = f + \sqrt{^2 + b^2\times f^{2\times c}} \times \varepsilon$$. y = observed, f = predicted, = additive sd, b = proportional/power sd, c = power exponent (1 proportional case). calcTables boolean determine foceiFit calculate tables. default TRUE compress object compressed items covMethod Method calculating covariance.  "analytic" (default) uses exact analytic observed-information R-matrix (reported \(R^{-1}\)) additionally returns residual Omega standard errors; covers FOCEI/FOCE fits additive, proportional, combined error, mu-referenced/covariate/structural parameters (non-mu-referenced etas), SD-scale inter-occasion variability, emits message falls back finite-difference Hessian anything scope (FO, nAGQ > 1, censoring, DV-transformed error, bounded-parameter transforms, structural theta shared two etas, non-SD iovXform, pure-proportional variance vanishes near-zero prediction).  finite-difference methods use R (Hessian) S (sum individual gradient cross-products empirical Bayes estimates): "r,s" sandwich (solve(R)%*%S%*%solve(R)), "r" Hessian-based (solve(R)), "s" cross-product-based (solve(S)), "" skip covariance step. adjObf boolean indicate objective function adjusted closer NONMEM's default objective function.  default TRUE ci Confidence level tables.  default 0.95 95% confidence. sigdig Optimization significant digits; controls inner/outer optimization tolerance (10^-sigdig), ODE solver tolerance (0.5*10^(-sigdig-2), 0.5*10^(-sigdig-1.5) sensitivity/steady-state liblsoda), boundary check tolerance (5*10^(-sigdig+1)). sigdigTable Significant digits final output table. specified, matches significant digits `sigdig` optimization algorithm.  `sigdig` NULL, use 3. eventSens Controls dosing/event-parameter (`alag`, `F`, `rate`, `dur`) sensitivities computed THETA/ETA gradients: `"jump"` (default) uses rxode2's analytic event sensitivities; `"fd"` uses legacy finite-difference behavior. ... Ignored parameters

code: # \donttest{ # A logit regression example with emax model  dsn <- data.frame(i=1:1000) dsn$time <- exp(rnorm(1000)) dsn$DV=rbinom(1000,1,exp(-1+dsn$time)/(1+exp(-1+dsn$time)))  mod <- function() {  ini({    E0 <- 0.5    Em <- 0.5    E50 <- 2    g <- fix(2)  })  model({    v <- E0+Em*time^g/(E50^g+time^g)    ll(bin) ~ DV * v - log(1 + exp(v))  }) }  fit2 <- nlmixr(mod, dsn, est="bobyqa") #>   #>   #>   #>   #> ℹ parameter labels from comments are typically ignored in non-interactive mode #> ℹ Need to run with the source intact to parse comments #> → pruning branches (`if`/`else`) of population log-likelihood model... #> ✔ done #> → loading llik model into symengine environment... #> → finding duplicate expressions in population log-likelihood model... #> → optimizing duplicate expressions in population log-likelihood model... #> ✔ done #>   #>   #> → calculating covariance #> ✔ done #> → loading into symengine environment... #> → pruning branches (`if`/`else`) of full model... #> ✔ done #> → finding duplicate expressions in EBE model... #> → optimizing duplicate expressions in EBE model... #> → compiling EBE model... #>   #>   #> ✔ done #> → Calculating residuals/tables #> ✔ done  print(fit2) #> ── nlmixr² log-likelihood bobyqa ── #>  #>           OBJF      AIC      BIC Log-likelihood Condition#(Cov) Condition#(Cor) #> lPop -714.4946 1129.382 1144.106      -561.6912        203.1204         52.1171 #>  #> ── Time (sec $time): ── #>  #>              setup   optimize covariance preprocess postprocess table compress #> elapsed 0.01864622 0.00213828  6.651e-06      0.036       0.012 0.026    0.001 #>            other #> elapsed 1.356209 #>  #> ── ($parFixed or $parFixedDf): ── #>  #>        Est.     SE  %RSE   Back-transformed(95%CI) BSV(SD) Shrink(SD)% #> E0  -0.7919 0.2718 34.33 -0.7919 (-1.325, -0.2591)                     #> Em    4.822  1.611 33.42       4.822 (1.664, 7.98)                     #> E50   2.282 0.9253 40.55     2.282 (0.4681, 4.095)                     #> g         2  FIXED FIXED                         2                     #>   #>   Covariance Type ($covMethod): r #>   Censoring ($censInformation): No censoring #>  #> ── Fit Data (object is a modified tibble): ── #> # A tibble: 1,000 × 5 #>   ID      TIME    DV  IPRED      v #>   <fct>  <dbl> <dbl>  <dbl>  <dbl> #> 1 1     0.0564     0 -0.375 -0.789 #> 2 1     0.0595     1 -1.16  -0.789 #> 3 1     0.0650     0 -0.375 -0.788 #> # ℹ 997 more rows  # you can also get the bobyqa output with  fit2$bobyqa #> parameter estimates: -0.79192990090358, 4.82215060488291, 2.28158116706737  #> objective: 561.691220766955  #> number of function evaluations: 201  # }

text: par named vector initial estimates setup nonlinear model solving environment. names parameter match names model run (`THETA[#]` required `modelInfo` argument) ui rxode2 ui model data rxode2 compatible data solving/setting modelInfo list `predOnly` (predictions-model terms `THETA[#]`/`DV`), `eventTheta` (0/1 per THETA flagging event-related parameters need Shi2021 finite differences, length `par`), `thetaGrad` (needed solveType != 1; gives value/gradient per THETA). See `ui$nlmSensModel` `ui$nlmRxModel` examples. control control structure; required: `rxControl`, `stickyRecalcN`, `maxOdeRecalc`, `odeRecalcFactor`. Optional: `solveType`, `eventType`, `shi21maxFD`, `shiErr`, `optimHessType`, `shi21maxHess`, `hessErr`, `useColor`, `printNcol`, `print`, `normType`, `scaleType`, `scaleCmin`, `scaleCmax`, `scaleTo`, `scaleC`, `gradTo` (default 0 missing). lower lower bounds, scaled present upper upper bounds, scaled present

title: Setup a nonlinear system for optimization — .nlmSetupEnv
text: nlm solve environment; key fields: `$par.ini`, `$lower`, `$upper`   (scaled), `$.ctl` (control structure).

title: Control Options for FOCEi — foceiControl
text: sigdig Optimization significant digits; controls inner/outer optimization tolerance (10^-sigdig), ODE solver tolerance (0.5*10^(-sigdig-2), 0.5*10^(-sigdig-1.5) sensitivity/steady-state liblsoda), boundary check tolerance (5*10^(-sigdig+1)). ... Ignored parameters epsilon Precision estimate n1qn1 optimization. maxInnerIterations Number iterations n1qn1 optimization. maxOuterIterations Maximum number L-BFGS-B optimization outer problem. n1qn1nsim Number function evaluations n1qn1 optimization. print Either scalar print-frequency (`0` = suppress, `1` (default) = every evaluation, `N` = every Nth), pre-built [iterPrintControl()] object. Equivalent `iterPrintControl(every = print, ncol = printNcol, useColor = useColor)`. printNcol Integer (`NULL`) parameter columns per row wrapping. `NULL` (default) uses `floor((getOption("width") - 23) / 12)`. scaleTo Scale initial parameter estimate value. default 1.  zero , scaling performed. scaleObjective Scale initial objective function value.  default 0 (meaning scale) normType Parameter normalization/scaling used get scaled initial values scaleType, form Vscaled = (Vunscaled-C1)/C2 (see Feature Scaling; rescale2 follows OptdesX manual): "rescale2" scales parameters (-1, 1); "rescale" (min-max) scales (0, 1); "mean" centers mean range (0, 1); "std" standardizes mean/sd; "len" scales unit (Euclidean) length; "constant" performs normalization (C1=0, C2=1). scaleType scaling scheme nlmixr2: "nlmixr2" (default) scales (current-init)*scaleC[] + scaleTo, scaleTo normType scales scaleC; "norm" uses simple scaling normType; "mult" scales multiplicatively current/init*scaleTo; "multAdd" scales linearly ((current-init)+scaleTo) parameters exponential block (e.g. exp(theta)) multiplicatively otherwise. scaleCmax Maximum value scaleC prevent overflow. scaleCmin Minimum value scaleC prevent underflow. scaleC Scaling constant used scaleType="nlmixr2"; specified, chosen parameter type keep gradient sizes similar log scale: `1` exp()-transformed/power/boxCox/ yeoJohnson parameters, `0.5*abs(est)` additive/proportional/ lognormal error parameters, `abs(1/digamma(est+1))` factorials, `log(abs(est))*abs(est)` log-scale parameters. May set explicitly per parameter defaults apply well. scaleC0 Number adjust scaling factor initial gradient zero. derivEps Forward difference tolerances (relative, absolute); step size h = abs(x)*derivEps[1] + derivEps[2]. derivMethod Derivative method outer problem: "switch", "central", "forward". "switch" starts forward toggles central abs(delta(OFV)) <= derivSwitchTol. derivSwitchTol tolerance switch forward central differences. covDerivMethod indicates method calculating derivatives calculating covariance components (Hessian S). covMethod Method calculating covariance.  "analytic" (default) uses exact analytic observed-information R-matrix (reported \(R^{-1}\)) additionally returns residual Omega standard errors; covers FOCEI/FOCE fits additive, proportional, combined error, mu-referenced/covariate/structural parameters (non-mu-referenced etas), SD-scale inter-occasion variability, emits message falls back finite-difference Hessian anything scope (FO, nAGQ > 1, censoring, DV-transformed error, bounded-parameter transforms, structural theta shared two etas, non-SD iovXform, pure-proportional variance vanishes near-zero prediction).  finite-difference methods use R (Hessian) S (sum individual gradient cross-products empirical Bayes estimates): "r,s" sandwich (solve(R)%*%S%*%solve(R)), "r" Hessian-based (solve(R)), "s" cross-product-based (solve(S)), "" skip covariance step. covSolveTol absolute/relative ODE tolerance covariance solves – augmented-sensitivity solves behind covMethod="analytic" perturbed solves behind finite-difference methods.  NULL (default) derives tight tolerance sigdig; supply number override . covFull controls shape fit$cov.  FALSE (default) installs structural-theta block (NONMEM-matched theta covariance, matching historical finite-difference fit$cov shape backwards compatibility); TRUE installs full theta + residual sigma + Omega covariance – assembled analytically covMethod="analytic", central finite differences objective parameter set finite-difference methods (perturbing Omega variance-covariance scale, per-parameter Gill (1983) step 5-point/4-point stencils foceiCalcR uses).  theta standard errors identical either way. fast TRUE, compute outer (population) gradient analytically Almquist (2015) sensitivity equations instead finite differences, use Eq-48 random-effect extrapolation next inner-problem starting values.  Requires analytic-scope model (single additive/proportional Gaussian endpoint); --scope models fall back finite-difference gradient message (linCmt() log-likelihood models downgrade fast=FALSE front).  unspecified, outer optimizer defaults "lbfgsb3c" (vs "nlminb" fast=FALSE); pairing fast=TRUE derivative-free outerOpt reverts fast=FALSE.  *f methods (e.g. foceif) default TRUE. hessEps double value representing epsilon Hessian calculation. used R matrix calculation. hessEpsLlik double value representing epsilon Hessian calculation focei generalized log-likelihood estimation.  used R matrix calculation. optimHessType Hessian type numeric-difference individual Hessians generalized log-likelihood estimation: "central" (matches R's `optimHess()`, default) "forward" (faster). optimHessCovType Hessian type numeric-difference individual Hessians used covariance step/final likelihood: "central" (accurate, used ) "forward". censOption Treatment second derivative censored (M2/M3/M4/BLQ) observations FOCEI family.  "gauss" (default) keeps historic uncensored Gauss-Newton curvature, matching common PMx tools; "laplace" uses exact censored second derivative objective (proper Laplace inner Hessian analytic covariance).  Accepted saemControl/nlmControl uniform interface inert – SAEM (stochastic EM) Laplace inner Hessian, NLM uses finite-difference Hessian already reflects censoring exactly. eventType Event gradient type dosing events; Can "central" "forward" eventSens Controls dosing/event-parameter (`alag`, `F`, `rate`, `dur`) sensitivities computed THETA/ETA gradients: `"jump"` (default) uses rxode2's analytic event sensitivities; `"fd"` uses legacy finite-difference behavior. centralDerivEps Central difference tolerances (relative, absolute); step size h = abs(x)*derivEps[1] + derivEps[2]. lbfgsLmm integer giving number BFGS updates retained "L-BFGS-B" method, defaults 7. lbfgsPgtol Projected-gradient convergence tolerance "L-BFGS-B": iteration stops max(| proj g_i |) <= lbfgsPgtol. Defaults `0` (check suppressed). lbfgsFactr Convergence factor "L-BFGS-B": converges objective reduction within lbfgsFactr * .Machine$double.eps. Default `1e10` (~4 sigdigs, 2e-6). eigen boolean indicating eigenvectors calculated include condition number calculation. diagXform Transformation used diagonal chol(solve(omega)) (FOCEi-estimated parameters): one "sqrt" (default), "log", "identity". iovXform Transformation used diagonal IOV: one "sd", "var", "logsd", "logvar". sumProd boolean indicating model change multiplication high precision multiplication sums high precision sums using PreciseSums package.  default FALSE. optExpression Optimize rxode2 expression speed calculation. default turned . literalFix boolean, substitute fixed population values literals re-adjust ui parameter estimates optimization; Default `TRUE`. literalFixRes boolean, substitute fixed population values literals re-adjust ui parameter estimates optimization; Default `TRUE`. ci Confidence level tables.  default 0.95 95% confidence. useColor Logical (`NULL`) emit ANSI bold/color escapes iteration print. `NULL` (default) defers [crayon::has_color()]. boundTol Tolerance boundary issues. calcTables boolean determine foceiFit calculate tables. default TRUE noAbort Boolean indicate abort FOCEi evaluation runs troubles.  (default TRUE) interaction Boolean indicate FOCEi used (TRUE) instead FOCE (FALSE) foce Controls FOCE (interaction = FALSE) evaluates     residual variance R inner objective; ignored FOCEi.  Either     "nonmem" (default) "foce+":  "nonmem" freezes R eta = 0 population     prediction holds constant across inner optimization, matching     NONMEM's FOCE.  Advantage: reproduces NONMEM FOCE objective standard     errors, ODE model agrees closed-form (linCmt)     equivalent.  Disadvantage: R ignores individual (conditional)     heteroscedasticity, can slightly less accurate     "foce+" proportional/combined error. "foce+" evaluates R current conditional     eta (live variance), keeping truncated FOCE     inner gradient.  Advantage: uses conditional variance     bit accurate NONMEM's FOCE cases.     Disadvantage: match NONMEM FOCE.  FOCE     behavior nlmixr2est 6.0.1 earlier.     use gradient eta like full focei     method, accurate focei. cholSEtol tolerance Generalized Cholesky Decomposition. Mirrors saemControl(muRefCovAlg=)/nlmeControl(muRefCovAlg=); foceiControl() takes effect muModel != "none". muModelTol Convergence tolerance mu-referenced-FOCEI-family "re-optimize etas, regress" cycle (muModel != "none"): repeats max mu-group theta change drops value muModelMaxCycles reached. muModelMaxCycles Maximum number "re-optimize etas, regress" cycles per outer iteration (see muModel, muModelTol). stateTrim Trim state amounts/concentrations value. shi21maxOuter maximum number steps optimization forward-difference step size.  zero, use instead Gill differences. shi21maxInner maximum central difference step size central differences optimization. covSmall Small number used compare covariance estimates (sandwich vs R/S matrix) rejecting one small final covariance estimate. adjLik `TRUE`, adjusts likelihood 2*pi constant nlmixr2's objective function otherwise omits (match NONMEM), closely matching nlme/SAS likelihood approximations. objective function always matches NONMEM regardless. gradTrim parameter adjust gradient |gradient| large. maxOdeRecalc Maximum number times reduce ODE tolerances try resolve system bad ODE solve. odeRecalcFactor ODE recalculation factor ODE solving goes bad, factor rtol/atol reduced gradCalcCentralSmall small number represents value |grad| < gradCalcCentralSmall forward differences switch central differences. gradCalcCentralLarge large number represents value |grad| > gradCalcCentralLarge forward differences switch central differences. etaNudge n1qn1 optimization ETA (starting zero) misbehaves, reset Hessian nudge ETA value, still move. Falls back etaNudge2, zero (stop optimizing) unsuccessful. etaNudge2 second eta nudge.  default qnorm(1-0.05/2)*sqrt(3/5), n=3 quadrature point (excluding zero) times 0.95% normal region nRetries FOCEi fit current parameter estimates, randomly sample new parameter estimates restart problem.  similar 'PsN' resampling. seed object specifying random number generator initialized resetThetaCheckPer represents objective function % percentage resetThetaP checked. etaMat Initial (final) ETA estimates; can also prior fit, whose final ETAs used initial values. default, uses last fit's ETAs supplied, else ETAs start zero (`NULL`). `NA` disables reuse prior fit. repeatGillMax tolerances reduced calculating initial Gill differences, Gill difference repeated maximum number times defined parameter. stickyRecalcN number bad ODE solves reducing atol/rtol rest problem. indTolRelax `TRUE` (default), subjects whose ODE solve produced NaN/Inf tolerances relaxed, relaxed tolerance persists across optimizer calls (sticky). `FALSE`, subjects tolerances relaxed retry tolerances reset afterward. gradProgressOfvTime time single objective function evaluation (seconds) start progress bars gradient evaluations addProp Type additive-plus-proportional error: `"combined1"`, standard deviations add: $$y = f + (+ b\times f^c) \times \varepsilon$$; `"combined2"`, variances add: $$y = f + \sqrt{^2 + b^2\times f^{2\times c}} \times \varepsilon$$. y = observed, f = predicted, = additive sd, b = proportional/power sd, c = power exponent (1 proportional case). badSolveObjfAdj objective function adjustment ODE system solved.  based individual bad solve. compress object compressed items rxControl `rxode2` ODE solving options fitting, created `rxControl()` sigdigTable Significant digits final output table. specified, matches significant digits `sigdig` optimization algorithm.  `sigdig` NULL, use 3. fallbackFD Fallback finite differences sensitivity equations solve. smatPer Percentage failed per-individual parameter gradients (replaced overall parameter gradient) total (`ntheta*nsub`) S matrix considered bad. sdLowerFact Factor multiplying estimate lower bound zero standard-deviation error parameter (add.sd, prop.sd, etc); e.g. estimate 0.15 lower bound 0 assumes lower bound 0.00015. `0` disables . zeroGradFirstReset `TRUE` (default), reset zero first gradient `sqrt(.Machine$double.eps)` instead erroring; `FALSE` errors; `NA` ignores last reset attempt. zeroGradRunReset `TRUE` (default), reset zero gradient encountered mid-run `sqrt(.Machine$double.eps)` instead erroring. zeroGradBobyqa `TRUE` (default), zero-gradient reset switches gradient-free bobyqa method; `NA` first zero gradient. mceta Monte Carlo sampling best initial ETA estimate (based `omega`): `-2` (default) uses Almquist (2015) Eq-48 extrapolation `eta^0 = eta* + (d eta*/d theta)(theta_new - theta_old)` analytic gradient supplies `d eta*/d theta` (`fast = TRUE`), accepting extrapolated eta within standardized-eta reset bound (else keeping last eta, resetting 0 also bound); `-1` jumps extrapolated eta eta=0, keeping better; `-2` `-1` fall back keeping last eta analytic `d eta*/d theta` available (`fast = FALSE`).  `0` uses eta=0 inner optimization; `n>0`, last eta, eta=0, n-1 etas sampled omega evaluated best (inner objective) used. warm Seeding n1qn1 inner-optimization Hessian: `"calc"` (default) warm-starts inner problem eta Hessian calculated objective function starting eta, calculating starting point unavailable; `"save"` uses classic self-initialized Hessian. nAGQ Number Gauss-Hermite adaptive quadrature points. `0` disables AGQ; `1` equivalent Laplace. Cost grows quickly ETAs: EBE found, expect `nAGQ^neta` (even `nAGQ`) `(nAGQ^neta)-1` (odd `nAGQ`) additional evaluations per subject. agqLow lower bound adaptive quadrature log-likelihood. default -Inf; original nlmixr's gnlmm -700. agqHi upper bound adaptive quadrature log-likelihood.  default Inf; original nlmixr's gnlmm 400. sensMethod Method used compute ODE parameter sensitivities: `"default"` (default) defers global option `getOption("nlmixr2est.adjoint")` (`"forward"` default); `"forward"` uses classic variational (forward) sensitivity ODEs; `"adjoint"` uses -engine discrete adjoint matching adjoint (`s`) method. boundedTransform `TRUE` (default), bounded parameters transformed unbounded optimization methods back-transformed final estimates. `FALSE` optimizes original scale bounds passed optimizer. `NA` transforms optimization skips final back-transform.

title: Control Options for FOCEi — foceiControl
text: Uses R's L-BFGS-B (optim) outer problem BFGS n1qn1 (restoring prior individual Hessian) inner problem, left unscaled since eta estimates start near zero. covariance step performed unscaled problem, condition number may differ scaled problem's.

Parameters used default `foceiConrol()` posthoc Logical indicating estimation method calculate `foce` posthoc predicted parameters. interaction Interaction term model; ignored fo fo Logical indicating estimation method FO (first order), controlled estimation method ignored.

what: Control options for the irlsagq estimation method — irlsagqControl
title: Control options for the irlsagq estimation method — irlsagqControl
text: Mu-referenced-FOCEI-family reweighted-regression (`"irls"`) variant adaptive Gauss-Hermite quadrature; see `foceiControl(muModel=)`.

title: Control options for the irlsagq estimation method — irlsagqControl
code: irlsagqControl(   sigdig = 3,   nAGQ = 2,   ...,   interaction = TRUE,   agqLow = -Inf,   agqHi = Inf,   muModel = c("irls", "lin", "none") )

title: Control options for the irlsagq estimation method — irlsagqControl
text: sigdig Optimization significant digits; controls inner/outer optimization tolerance (10^-sigdig), ODE solver tolerance (0.5*10^(-sigdig-2), 0.5*10^(-sigdig-1.5) sensitivity/steady-state liblsoda), boundary check tolerance (5*10^(-sigdig+1)). nAGQ Number Gauss-Hermite adaptive quadrature points. `0` disables AGQ; `1` equivalent Laplace. Cost grows quickly ETAs: EBE found, expect `nAGQ^neta` (even `nAGQ`) `(nAGQ^neta)-1` (odd `nAGQ`) additional evaluations per subject. ... Parameters used default `foceiControl()` interaction boolean, Interaction term model, case default `TRUE`; adaptive quadrature, normal distribution Hessian calculated foce() approximation agqLow lower bound adaptive quadrature log-likelihood. default -Inf; original nlmixr's gnlmm -700. agqHi upper bound adaptive quadrature log-likelihood.  default Inf; original nlmixr's gnlmm 400. muModel Selects regression variant; `irlsagqControl()` always `"irls"` changed – use `muagqControl()` closed-form OLS variant.

title: Control options for the irlsfocei estimation method — irlsfoceiControl
Control options for the irlsfocei estimation method — irlsfoceiControl
code: irlsfoceiControl(sigdig = 3, ..., muModel = c("irls", "lin", "none"))

title: Control options for the irlsfocei estimation method — irlsfoceiControl
text: sigdig Optimization significant digits; controls inner/outer optimization tolerance (10^-sigdig), ODE solver tolerance (0.5*10^(-sigdig-2), 0.5*10^(-sigdig-1.5) sensitivity/steady-state liblsoda), boundary check tolerance (5*10^(-sigdig+1)). ... Parameters used default `foceiControl()` muModel Selects regression variant; `irlsfoceiControl()` always `"irls"` changed – use `mufoceiControl()` closed-form OLS variant.

what: Control options for the irlsfocep (IRLS FOCE+) estimation method — irlsfocepControl
title: Control options for the irlsfocep (IRLS FOCE+) estimation method — irlsfocepControl
text: Mu-referenced-FOCEI-family reweighted-regression (`"irls"`) variant FOCE+ (interaction, live conditional residual variance R); `irlsfoce` `foce = "foce+"` forced (see `foceiControl(foce=)`).

title: Control options for the irlsfocep (IRLS FOCE+) estimation method — irlsfocepControl
code: irlsfocepControl(   sigdig = 3,   ...,   interaction = FALSE,   muModel = c("irls", "lin", "none"),   foce = "foce+" )

title: Control options for the irlsfocep (IRLS FOCE+) estimation method — irlsfocepControl
text: sigdig Optimization significant digits; controls inner/outer optimization tolerance (10^-sigdig), ODE solver tolerance (0.5*10^(-sigdig-2), 0.5*10^(-sigdig-1.5) sensitivity/steady-state liblsoda), boundary check tolerance (5*10^(-sigdig+1)). ... Parameters used default `foceiControl()` interaction Interaction term model, case default `FALSE`; changed, use `irlsfocei` instead muModel Selects regression variant; `irlsfocepControl()` always `"irls"` changed – use `mufocepControl()` closed-form OLS variant. foce FOCE residual-variance mode; `irlsfocepControl()` always `"foce+"` changed – use `irlsfoceControl()` `"nonmem"`

what: Control options for the irlslaplace estimation method — irlslaplaceControl
title: Control options for the irlslaplace estimation method — irlslaplaceControl
text: Mu-referenced-FOCEI-family reweighted-regression (`"irls"`) variant Laplace method (`nAGQ=1`); see `foceiControl(muModel=)`.

title: Control options for the irlslaplace estimation method — irlslaplaceControl
code: irlslaplaceControl(   sigdig = 3,   ...,   nAGQ = 1,   muModel = c("irls", "lin", "none") )

title: Control options for the irlslaplace estimation method — irlslaplaceControl
text: sigdig Optimization significant digits; controls inner/outer optimization tolerance (10^-sigdig), ODE solver tolerance (0.5*10^(-sigdig-2), 0.5*10^(-sigdig-1.5) sensitivity/steady-state liblsoda), boundary check tolerance (5*10^(-sigdig+1)). ... Parameters used default `foceiControl()` nAGQ Number Gauss-Hermite adaptive quadrature points. `0` disables AGQ; `1` equivalent Laplace. Cost grows quickly ETAs: EBE found, expect `nAGQ^neta` (even `nAGQ`) `(nAGQ^neta)-1` (odd `nAGQ`) additional evaluations per subject. muModel Selects regression variant; `irlslaplaceControl()` always `"irls"` changed – use `mulaplaceControl()` closed-form OLS variant.

title: Control iteration-time print formatting — iterPrintControl
text: Bundles options controlling iteration progress output emitted `nlmixr2` estimators. Pass `print` argument `*Control()` function; scalar form (`print = N`) still works wrapped `iterPrintControl()` internally.

title: Control iteration-time print formatting — iterPrintControl
code: iterPrintControl(   every = 1L,   ncol = NULL,   headerEvery = NULL,   useColor = NULL,   simple = FALSE )

title: Control iteration-time print formatting — iterPrintControl
text: every Integer. Print one iteration row every `every` parameter evaluations; `0` suppresses output. Defaults `1L`. ncol Integer `NULL`. Parameter columns per row wrapping. `NULL` (default) uses `floor((getOption("width") - 23) / 12)`. headerEvery Integer `NULL`. Re-emit column header every `headerEvery` parameter-print events; `0` prints fit start. `NULL` (default) uses `10L`. useColor Logical (`NULL`) emit ANSI bold/color escapes iteration print. `NULL` (default) defers [crayon::has_color()]. simple Logical. `TRUE`, print single row per iteration, suppressing unscaled (`U`) / back-transformed (`X`) rows. Defaults `FALSE`.

title: Control iteration-time print formatting — iterPrintControl
text: list validated, defaulted iteration-print   options.  class `"iterPrintControl"` outer `*Control()`   functions can distinguish pre-built object scalar   `print = N`.

title: Control iteration-time print formatting — iterPrintControl
Control iteration-time print formatting — iterPrintControl
code: iterPrintControl() #> $every #> [1] 1 #>  #> $ncol #> [1] 4 #>  #> $headerEvery #> [1] 10 #>  #> $useColor #> [1] TRUE #>  #> $simple #> [1] FALSE #>  #> attr(,"class") #> [1] "iterPrintControl" "list"             iterPrintControl(every = 5, headerEvery = 0) #> $every #> [1] 5 #>  #> $ncol #> [1] 4 #>  #> $headerEvery #> [1] 0 #>  #> $useColor #> [1] TRUE #>  #> $simple #> [1] FALSE #>  #> attr(,"class") #> [1] "iterPrintControl" "list"

title: Iteration-print configuration parameters (documentation stub) — iterPrintParams
text: Shared `@param` docs iteration-print formatting, used scalar `*Control()` arguments [iterPrintControl()] via `@inheritParams`.

title: Iteration-print configuration parameters (documentation stub) — iterPrintParams
text: print Either scalar print-frequency (`0` = suppress, `1` (default) = every evaluation, `N` = every Nth), pre-built [iterPrintControl()] object. Equivalent `iterPrintControl(every = print, ncol = printNcol, useColor = useColor)`. printNcol Integer (`NULL`) parameter columns per row wrapping. `NULL` (default) uses `floor((getOption("width") - 23) / 12)`. every Integer. Print one iteration row every `every` parameter evaluations; `0` suppresses output. Defaults `1L`. ncol Integer `NULL`. Parameter columns per row wrapping. `NULL` (default) uses `floor((getOption("width") - 23) / 12)`. headerEvery Integer `NULL`. Re-emit column header every `headerEvery` parameter-print events; `0` prints fit start. `NULL` (default) uses `10L`. useColor Logical (`NULL`) emit ANSI bold/color escapes iteration print. `NULL` (default) defers [crayon::has_color()]. simple Logical. `TRUE`, print single row per iteration, suppressing unscaled (`U`) / back-transformed (`X`) rows. Defaults `FALSE`.

title: Iteration-print configuration parameters (documentation stub) — iterPrintParams

what: Control options for the Laplace estimation method — laplaceControl
title: Control options for the Laplace estimation method — laplaceControl
text: control options adaptive Gauss-Hermite quadrature likelihood.  Note nAGQ=1 Laplace method.

title: Control options for the Laplace estimation method — laplaceControl
code: laplaceControl(sigdig = 3, ..., nAGQ = 1)

title: Control options for the Laplace estimation method — laplaceControl
text: sigdig Optimization significant digits; controls inner/outer optimization tolerance (10^-sigdig), ODE solver tolerance (0.5*10^(-sigdig-2), 0.5*10^(-sigdig-1.5) sensitivity/steady-state liblsoda), boundary check tolerance (5*10^(-sigdig+1)). ... Parameters used default `foceiControl()` nAGQ Number Gauss-Hermite adaptive quadrature points. `0` disables AGQ; `1` equivalent Laplace. Cost grows quickly ETAs: EBE found, expect `nAGQ^neta` (even `nAGQ`) `(nAGQ^neta)-1` (odd `nAGQ`) additional evaluations per subject.

title: Control options for the Laplace estimation method — laplaceControl
Control options for the Laplace estimation method — laplaceControl
text: method can made closely matches NONMEM-style Laplace estimation requesting log-likelihood STAN well numerically calculated Hessian matrix. done adding `+dnorm()` model normal end-points.

[1] 1 #>  #> $agqHi #> [1] Inf #>  #> $agqLow #> [1] -Inf #>  #> $sensMethod #> [1] "default" #>  #> $boundedTransform #> [1] TRUE #>  #> attr(,"class") #> [1] "laplaceControl"  # Use adaptive quadrature  # x =  Litter size after 21 days, and the modeled value  r <- rats r$dv <- r$x  # Time is not used in this model, but it is required in nlmixr2 # currently, add a dummy value  r$time <- 0  f <- function() {   ini({     t1 <- 1     t2 <- 1     t3 <- 1     eta1 ~ 1   })   model({     lp <- t1 * x1 + t2 * x2 + (x1 + x2*t3) * eta1     p <- pnorm(lp)     m1 <- m # need to add outside of model specification     x ~ dbinom(m1, p)   }) }  fit <- nlmixr(f, r, est="laplace") #>   #>   #>   #>   #> ℹ parameter labels from comments are typically ignored in non-interactive mode #> ℹ Need to run with the source intact to parse comments #> Warning: some etas defaulted to non-mu referenced, possible parsing error: eta1 #> as a work-around try putting the mu-referenced expression on a simple line #> Warning: some etas defaulted to non-mu referenced, possible parsing error: eta1 #> as a work-around try putting the mu-referenced expression on a simple line #> covType="analytic": a model with no residual error is out of analytic-covariance scope; using the finite-difference covariance instead #> → Calculating residuals/tables #> ✔ done   p <- pump  p$dv <- p$y p$time <- 0 # dummy time  f <- function() {   ini({     t1 <- 1     t2 <- 1     t3 <- 1     t4 <- 1     eta1 ~ 1   })   model({     if (group == 1) {        lp <- t1 + t2 * logtstd     } else {        lp <- t3 + t4 * logtstd     }     lp <- lp + eta1     lam <- exp(lp)     y ~ dpois(lam)   }) }  fit <- nlmixr(f, p, est="laplace") #>   #>   #>   #>   #> ℹ parameter labels from comments are typically ignored in non-interactive mode #> ℹ Need to run with the source intact to parse comments #> covType="analytic": a model with no residual error is out of analytic-covariance scope; using the finite-difference covariance instead #> → Calculating residuals/tables #> ✔ done  # }

title: Control for lbfgsb3c estimation method in nlmixr2 — lbfgsb3cControl
text: trace positive, print tracing information; higher values give detail (see source "L-BFGS-B" trace levels). factr Convergence tolerance factor "L-BFGS-B"; converges objective reduction within factor machine tolerance (default 1e7, .e. ~1e-8). pgtol Tolerance projected gradient "L-BFGS-B"; 0 (default) suppresses check. abstol Absolute x-value tolerance "L-BFGS-B"; 0 (default) suppresses check. reltol Relative x-value tolerance "L-BFGS-B"; 0 (default) suppresses check. lmm Number BFGS updates retained "L-BFGS-B" (default 5). maxit maximum number iterations. returnLbfgsb3c return lbfgsb3c output instead nlmixr2 fit stickyRecalcN number bad ODE solves reducing atol/rtol rest problem. maxOdeRecalc Maximum number times reduce ODE tolerances try resolve system bad ODE solve. odeRecalcFactor ODE recalculation factor ODE solving goes bad, factor rtol/atol reduced indTolRelax `TRUE` (default), subjects whose ODE solve produced NaN/Inf tolerances relaxed, relaxed tolerance persists across optimizer calls (sticky). `FALSE`, subjects tolerances relaxed retry tolerances reset afterward. useColor Logical (`NULL`) emit ANSI bold/color escapes iteration print. `NULL` (default) defers [crayon::has_color()]. printNcol Integer (`NULL`) parameter columns per row wrapping. `NULL` (default) uses `floor((getOption("width") - 23) / 12)`. print Either scalar print-frequency (`0` = suppress, `1` (default) = every evaluation, `N` = every Nth), pre-built [iterPrintControl()] object. Equivalent `iterPrintControl(every = print, ncol = printNcol, useColor = useColor)`. normType Parameter normalization/scaling used get scaled initial values scaleType, form Vscaled = (Vunscaled-C1)/C2 (see Feature Scaling; rescale2 follows OptdesX manual): "rescale2" scales parameters (-1, 1); "rescale" (min-max) scales (0, 1); "mean" centers mean range (0, 1); "std" standardizes mean/sd; "len" scales unit (Euclidean) length; "constant" performs normalization (C1=0, C2=1). scaleType scaling scheme nlmixr2: "nlmixr2" (default) scales (current-init)*scaleC[] + scaleTo, scaleTo normType scales scaleC; "norm" uses simple scaling normType; "mult" scales multiplicatively current/init*scaleTo; "multAdd" scales linearly ((current-init)+scaleTo) parameters exponential block (e.g. exp(theta)) multiplicatively otherwise. scaleCmax Maximum value scaleC prevent overflow. scaleCmin Minimum value scaleC prevent underflow. scaleC Scaling constant used scaleType="nlmixr2"; specified, chosen parameter type keep gradient sizes similar log scale: `1` exp()-transformed/power/boxCox/ yeoJohnson parameters, `0.5*abs(est)` additive/proportional/ lognormal error parameters, `abs(1/digamma(est+1))` factorials, `log(abs(est))*abs(est)` log-scale parameters. May set explicitly per parameter defaults apply well. scaleTo Scale initial parameter estimate value. default 1.  zero , scaling performed. gradTo factor gradient scaled optimizing.  works scaleType="nlmixr2". rxControl `rxode2` ODE solving options fitting, created `rxControl()` optExpression Optimize rxode2 expression speed calculation. default turned . sumProd boolean indicating model change multiplication high precision multiplication sums high precision sums using PreciseSums package.  default FALSE. literalFix boolean, substitute fixed population values literals re-adjust ui parameter estimates optimization; Default `TRUE`. literalFixRes boolean, substitute fixed population values literals re-adjust ui parameter estimates optimization; Default `TRUE`. addProp Type additive-plus-proportional error: `"combined1"`, standard deviations add: $$y = f + (+ b\times f^c) \times \varepsilon$$; `"combined2"`, variances add: $$y = f + \sqrt{^2 + b^2\times f^{2\times c}} \times \varepsilon$$. y = observed, f = predicted, = additive sd, b = proportional/power sd, c = power exponent (1 proportional case). eventSens Controls dosing/event-parameter (`alag`, `F`, `rate`, `dur`) sensitivities computed THETA/ETA gradients: `"jump"` (default) uses rxode2's analytic event sensitivities; `"fd"` uses legacy finite-difference behavior. sensMethod Method used compute ODE parameter sensitivities: `"default"` (default) defers global option `getOption("nlmixr2est.adjoint")` (`"forward"` default); `"forward"` uses classic variational (forward) sensitivity ODEs; `"adjoint"` uses -engine discrete adjoint matching adjoint (`s`) method. calcTables boolean determine foceiFit calculate tables. default TRUE compress object compressed items covMethod Method calculating covariance.  "analytic" (default) uses exact analytic observed-information R-matrix (reported \(R^{-1}\)) additionally returns residual Omega standard errors; covers FOCEI/FOCE fits additive, proportional, combined error, mu-referenced/covariate/structural parameters (non-mu-referenced etas), SD-scale inter-occasion variability, emits message falls back finite-difference Hessian anything scope (FO, nAGQ > 1, censoring, DV-transformed error, bounded-parameter transforms, structural theta shared two etas, non-SD iovXform, pure-proportional variance vanishes near-zero prediction).  finite-difference methods use R (Hessian) S (sum individual gradient cross-products empirical Bayes estimates): "r,s" sandwich (solve(R)%*%S%*%solve(R)), "r" Hessian-based (solve(R)), "s" cross-product-based (solve(S)), "" skip covariance step. adjObf boolean indicate objective function adjusted closer NONMEM's default objective function.  default TRUE ci Confidence level tables.  default 0.95 95% confidence. sigdig Optimization significant digits; controls inner/outer optimization tolerance (10^-sigdig), ODE solver tolerance (0.5*10^(-sigdig-2), 0.5*10^(-sigdig-1.5) sensitivity/steady-state liblsoda), boundary check tolerance (5*10^(-sigdig+1)). sigdigTable Significant digits final output table. specified, matches significant digits `sigdig` optimization algorithm.  `sigdig` NULL, use 3. ... Ignored parameters

title: Control options for the muagq estimation method — muagqControl
text: sigdig Optimization significant digits; controls inner/outer optimization tolerance (10^-sigdig), ODE solver tolerance (0.5*10^(-sigdig-2), 0.5*10^(-sigdig-1.5) sensitivity/steady-state liblsoda), boundary check tolerance (5*10^(-sigdig+1)). nAGQ Number Gauss-Hermite adaptive quadrature points. `0` disables AGQ; `1` equivalent Laplace. Cost grows quickly ETAs: EBE found, expect `nAGQ^neta` (even `nAGQ`) `(nAGQ^neta)-1` (odd `nAGQ`) additional evaluations per subject. ... Parameters used default `foceiControl()` interaction boolean, Interaction term model, case default `TRUE`; adaptive quadrature, normal distribution Hessian calculated foce() approximation agqLow lower bound adaptive quadrature log-likelihood. default -Inf; original nlmixr's gnlmm -700. agqHi upper bound adaptive quadrature log-likelihood.  default Inf; original nlmixr's gnlmm 400. muModel Selects regression variant; `muagqControl()` always `"lin"` changed – use `irlsagqControl()` IRLS variant.

what: Control options for the mufocep (mu-referenced FOCE+) estimation method — mufocepControl
title: Control options for the mufocep (mu-referenced FOCE+) estimation method — mufocepControl
text: Mu-referenced-FOCEI-family closed-form-regression (`"lin"`) variant FOCE+ (interaction, live conditional residual variance R); `mufoce` `foce = "foce+"` forced (see `foceiControl(foce=)`).

title: Control options for the mufocep (mu-referenced FOCE+) estimation method — mufocepControl
code: mufocepControl(   sigdig = 3,   ...,   interaction = FALSE,   muModel = c("lin", "irls", "none"),   foce = "foce+" )

title: Control options for the mufocep (mu-referenced FOCE+) estimation method — mufocepControl
text: sigdig Optimization significant digits; controls inner/outer optimization tolerance (10^-sigdig), ODE solver tolerance (0.5*10^(-sigdig-2), 0.5*10^(-sigdig-1.5) sensitivity/steady-state liblsoda), boundary check tolerance (5*10^(-sigdig+1)). ... Parameters used default `foceiControl()` interaction Interaction term model, case default `FALSE`; changed, use `mufocei` instead muModel Selects regression variant; `mufocepControl()` always `"lin"` changed – use `irlsfocepControl()` IRLS variant. foce FOCE residual-variance mode; `mufocepControl()` always `"foce+"` changed – use `mufoceControl()` `"nonmem"`

title: Control options for the mulaplace estimation method — mulaplaceControl
text: Mu-referenced-FOCEI-family closed-form-regression (`"lin"`) variant Laplace method (`nAGQ=1`); see `foceiControl(muModel=)`.

title: Control options for the mulaplace estimation method — mulaplaceControl
code: mulaplaceControl(sigdig = 3, ..., nAGQ = 1, muModel = c("lin", "irls", "none"))

title: Control options for the mulaplace estimation method — mulaplaceControl
text: sigdig Optimization significant digits; controls inner/outer optimization tolerance (10^-sigdig), ODE solver tolerance (0.5*10^(-sigdig-2), 0.5*10^(-sigdig-1.5) sensitivity/steady-state liblsoda), boundary check tolerance (5*10^(-sigdig+1)). ... Parameters used default `foceiControl()` nAGQ Number Gauss-Hermite adaptive quadrature points. `0` disables AGQ; `1` equivalent Laplace. Cost grows quickly ETAs: EBE found, expect `nAGQ^neta` (even `nAGQ`) `(nAGQ^neta)-1` (odd `nAGQ`) additional evaluations per subject. muModel Selects regression variant; `mulaplaceControl()` always `"lin"` changed – use `irlslaplaceControl()` IRLS variant.

title: Control for n1qn1 estimation method in nlmixr2 — n1qn1Control
text: epsilon Precision estimate n1qn1 optimization. max_iterations Number iterations nsim Number function evaluations imp Verbosity messages. print.functions Boolean control function value parameter estimates echoed every time function called. returnN1qn1 return n1qn1 output instead nlmixr2 fit stickyRecalcN number bad ODE solves reducing atol/rtol rest problem. maxOdeRecalc Maximum number times reduce ODE tolerances try resolve system bad ODE solve. odeRecalcFactor ODE recalculation factor ODE solving goes bad, factor rtol/atol reduced indTolRelax `TRUE` (default), subjects whose ODE solve produced NaN/Inf tolerances relaxed, relaxed tolerance persists across optimizer calls (sticky). `FALSE`, subjects tolerances relaxed retry tolerances reset afterward. useColor Logical (`NULL`) emit ANSI bold/color escapes iteration print. `NULL` (default) defers [crayon::has_color()]. printNcol Integer (`NULL`) parameter columns per row wrapping. `NULL` (default) uses `floor((getOption("width") - 23) / 12)`. print Either scalar print-frequency (`0` = suppress, `1` (default) = every evaluation, `N` = every Nth), pre-built [iterPrintControl()] object. Equivalent `iterPrintControl(every = print, ncol = printNcol, useColor = useColor)`. normType Parameter normalization/scaling used get scaled initial values scaleType, form Vscaled = (Vunscaled-C1)/C2 (see Feature Scaling; rescale2 follows OptdesX manual): "rescale2" scales parameters (-1, 1); "rescale" (min-max) scales (0, 1); "mean" centers mean range (0, 1); "std" standardizes mean/sd; "len" scales unit (Euclidean) length; "constant" performs normalization (C1=0, C2=1). scaleType scaling scheme nlmixr2: "nlmixr2" (default) scales (current-init)*scaleC[] + scaleTo, scaleTo normType scales scaleC; "norm" uses simple scaling normType; "mult" scales multiplicatively current/init*scaleTo; "multAdd" scales linearly ((current-init)+scaleTo) parameters exponential block (e.g. exp(theta)) multiplicatively otherwise. scaleCmax Maximum value scaleC prevent overflow. scaleCmin Minimum value scaleC prevent underflow. scaleC Scaling constant used scaleType="nlmixr2"; specified, chosen parameter type keep gradient sizes similar log scale: `1` exp()-transformed/power/boxCox/ yeoJohnson parameters, `0.5*abs(est)` additive/proportional/ lognormal error parameters, `abs(1/digamma(est+1))` factorials, `log(abs(est))*abs(est)` log-scale parameters. May set explicitly per parameter defaults apply well. scaleTo Scale initial parameter estimate value. default 1.  zero , scaling performed. gradTo factor gradient scaled optimizing.  works scaleType="nlmixr2". rxControl `rxode2` ODE solving options fitting, created `rxControl()` optExpression Optimize rxode2 expression speed calculation. default turned . sumProd boolean indicating model change multiplication high precision multiplication sums high precision sums using PreciseSums package.  default FALSE. literalFix boolean, substitute fixed population values literals re-adjust ui parameter estimates optimization; Default `TRUE`. literalFixRes boolean, substitute fixed population values literals re-adjust ui parameter estimates optimization; Default `TRUE`. addProp Type additive-plus-proportional error: `"combined1"`, standard deviations add: $$y = f + (+ b\times f^c) \times \varepsilon$$; `"combined2"`, variances add: $$y = f + \sqrt{^2 + b^2\times f^{2\times c}} \times \varepsilon$$. y = observed, f = predicted, = additive sd, b = proportional/power sd, c = power exponent (1 proportional case). eventSens Controls dosing/event-parameter (`alag`, `F`, `rate`, `dur`) sensitivities computed THETA/ETA gradients: `"jump"` (default) uses rxode2's analytic event sensitivities; `"fd"` uses legacy finite-difference behavior. sensMethod Method used compute ODE parameter sensitivities: `"default"` (default) defers global option `getOption("nlmixr2est.adjoint")` (`"forward"` default); `"forward"` uses classic variational (forward) sensitivity ODEs; `"adjoint"` uses -engine discrete adjoint matching adjoint (`s`) method. calcTables boolean determine foceiFit calculate tables. default TRUE compress object compressed items covMethod Method calculating covariance.  "analytic" (default) uses exact analytic observed-information R-matrix (reported \(R^{-1}\)) additionally returns residual Omega standard errors; covers FOCEI/FOCE fits additive, proportional, combined error, mu-referenced/covariate/structural parameters (non-mu-referenced etas), SD-scale inter-occasion variability, emits message falls back finite-difference Hessian anything scope (FO, nAGQ > 1, censoring, DV-transformed error, bounded-parameter transforms, structural theta shared two etas, non-SD iovXform, pure-proportional variance vanishes near-zero prediction).  finite-difference methods use R (Hessian) S (sum individual gradient cross-products empirical Bayes estimates): "r,s" sandwich (solve(R)%*%S%*%solve(R)), "r" Hessian-based (solve(R)), "s" cross-product-based (solve(S)), "" skip covariance step. adjObf boolean indicate objective function adjusted closer NONMEM's default objective function.  default TRUE ci Confidence level tables.  default 0.95 95% confidence. sigdig Optimization significant digits; controls inner/outer optimization tolerance (10^-sigdig), ODE solver tolerance (0.5*10^(-sigdig-2), 0.5*10^(-sigdig-1.5) sensitivity/steady-state liblsoda), boundary check tolerance (5*10^(-sigdig+1)). sigdigTable Significant digits final output table. specified, matches significant digits `sigdig` optimization algorithm.  `sigdig` NULL, use 3. boundedTransform `TRUE` (default), bounded parameters transformed unbounded optimization methods back-transformed final estimates. `FALSE` optimizes original scale bounds passed optimizer. `NA` transforms optimization skips final back-transform. ...
