title: Control for uobyqa estimation method in nlmixr2 — uobyqaControl
text: npt Number points bobyqa's quadratic approximation objective; must `[n+2, (n+1)(n+2)/2]`. Defaults `2*n + 1`. (bobyqa) rhobeg Initial trust region radius bobyqa outer optimizer (`rhoend`, must satisfy `0 < rhoend < rhobeg`). Default `0.2` (20 `abs(upper-lower)/2`. (bobyqa) rhoend Final trust region radius. defined, `10^(-sigdig-1)` used. (bobyqa) iprint Controls amount printing (`0`=none, `1`=start/end , `2`=new rho, `3`=every function evaluation, `>3`=every `iprint` evaluations). Default `0`. maxfun maximum allowed number function evaluations. exceeded, method terminate. returnUobyqa return uobyqa output instead nlmixr2 fit stickyRecalcN number bad ODE solves reducing atol/rtol rest problem. maxOdeRecalc Maximum number times reduce ODE tolerances try resolve system bad ODE solve. odeRecalcFactor ODE recalculation factor ODE solving goes bad, factor rtol/atol reduced indTolRelax `TRUE` (default), subjects whose ODE solve produced NaN/Inf tolerances relaxed, relaxed tolerance persists across optimizer calls (sticky). `FALSE`, subjects tolerances relaxed retry tolerances reset afterward. useColor Logical (`NULL`) emit ANSI bold/color escapes iteration print. `NULL` (default) defers [crayon::has_color()]. printNcol Integer (`NULL`) parameter columns per row wrapping. `NULL` (default) uses `floor((getOption("width") - 23) / 12)`. print Either scalar print-frequency (`0` = suppress, `1` (default) = every evaluation, `N` = every Nth), pre-built [iterPrintControl()] object. Equivalent `iterPrintControl(every = print, ncol = printNcol, useColor = useColor)`. normType Parameter normalization/scaling used get scaled initial values scaleType, form Vscaled = (Vunscaled-C1)/C2 (see Feature Scaling; rescale2 follows OptdesX manual): "rescale2" scales parameters (-1, 1); "rescale" (min-max) scales (0, 1); "mean" centers mean range (0, 1); "std" standardizes mean/sd; "len" scales unit (Euclidean) length; "constant" performs normalization (C1=0, C2=1). scaleType scaling scheme nlmixr2: "nlmixr2" (default) scales (current-init)*scaleC[] + scaleTo, scaleTo normType scales scaleC; "norm" uses simple scaling normType; "mult" scales multiplicatively current/init*scaleTo; "multAdd" scales linearly ((current-init)+scaleTo) parameters exponential block (e.g. exp(theta)) multiplicatively otherwise. scaleCmax Maximum value scaleC prevent overflow. scaleCmin Minimum value scaleC prevent underflow. scaleC Scaling constant used scaleType="nlmixr2"; specified, chosen parameter type keep gradient sizes similar log scale: `1` exp()-transformed/power/boxCox/ yeoJohnson parameters, `0.5*abs(est)` additive/proportional/ lognormal error parameters, `abs(1/digamma(est+1))` factorials, `log(abs(est))*abs(est)` log-scale parameters. May set explicitly per parameter defaults apply well. scaleTo Scale initial parameter estimate value. default 1.  zero , scaling performed. rxControl `rxode2` ODE solving options fitting, created `rxControl()` optExpression Optimize rxode2 expression speed calculation. default turned . sumProd boolean indicating model change multiplication high precision multiplication sums high precision sums using PreciseSums package.  default FALSE. literalFix boolean, substitute fixed population values literals re-adjust ui parameter estimates optimization; Default `TRUE`. literalFixRes boolean, substitute fixed population values literals re-adjust ui parameter estimates optimization; Default `TRUE`. addProp Type additive-plus-proportional error: `"combined1"`, standard deviations add: $$y = f + (+ b\times f^c) \times \varepsilon$$; `"combined2"`, variances add: $$y = f + \sqrt{^2 + b^2\times f^{2\times c}} \times \varepsilon$$. y = observed, f = predicted, = additive sd, b = proportional/power sd, c = power exponent (1 proportional case). calcTables boolean determine foceiFit calculate tables. default TRUE compress object compressed items covMethod Method calculating covariance.  "analytic" (default) uses exact analytic observed-information R-matrix (reported \(R^{-1}\)) additionally returns residual Omega standard errors; covers FOCEI/FOCE fits additive, proportional, combined error, mu-referenced/covariate/structural parameters (non-mu-referenced etas), SD-scale inter-occasion variability, emits message falls back finite-difference Hessian anything scope (FO, nAGQ > 1, censoring, DV-transformed error, bounded-parameter transforms, structural theta shared two etas, non-SD iovXform, pure-proportional variance vanishes near-zero prediction).  finite-difference methods use R (Hessian) S (sum individual gradient cross-products empirical Bayes estimates): "r,s" sandwich (solve(R)%*%S%*%solve(R)), "r" Hessian-based (solve(R)), "s" cross-product-based (solve(S)), "" skip covariance step. adjObf boolean indicate objective function adjusted closer NONMEM's default objective function.  default TRUE ci Confidence level tables.  default 0.95 95% confidence. sigdig Optimization significant digits; controls inner/outer optimization tolerance (10^-sigdig), ODE solver tolerance (0.5*10^(-sigdig-2), 0.5*10^(-sigdig-1.5) sensitivity/steady-state liblsoda), boundary check tolerance (5*10^(-sigdig+1)). sigdigTable Significant digits final output table. specified, matches significant digits `sigdig` optimization algorithm.  `sigdig` NULL, use 3. boundedTransform `TRUE` (default), bounded parameters transformed unbounded optimization methods back-transformed final estimates. `FALSE` optimizes original scale bounds passed optimizer. `NA` transforms optimization skips final back-transform. eventSens Controls dosing/event-parameter (`alag`, `F`, `rate`, `dur`) sensitivities computed THETA/ETA gradients: `"jump"` (default) uses rxode2's analytic event sensitivities; `"fd"` uses legacy finite-difference behavior. ... code: # \donttest{ # A logit regression example with emax model  dsn <- data.frame(i=1:1000) dsn$time <- exp(rnorm(1000)) dsn$DV=rbinom(1000,1,exp(-1+dsn$time)/(1+exp(-1+dsn$time)))  mod <- function() {  ini({    E0 <- 0.5    Em <- 0.5    E50 <- 2    g <- fix(2)  })  model({    v <- E0+Em*time^g/(E50^g+time^g)    ll(bin) ~ DV * v - log(1 + exp(v))  }) }  fit2 <- nlmixr(mod, dsn, est="uobyqa") #>   #>   #>   #>   #> ℹ parameter labels from comments are typically ignored in non-interactive mode #> ℹ Need to run with the source intact to parse comments #> → pruning branches (`if`/`else`) of population log-likelihood model... #> ✔ done #> → loading llik model into symengine environment... #> → finding duplicate expressions in population log-likelihood model... #> → optimizing duplicate expressions in population log-likelihood model... #> ✔ done #>   #>   #> → calculating covariance #> ✔ done #> → loading into symengine environment... #> → pruning branches (`if`/`else`) of full model... #> ✔ done #> → finding duplicate expressions in EBE model... #> → optimizing duplicate expressions in EBE model... #> → compiling EBE model... #>   #>   #> ✔ done #> → Calculating residuals/tables #> ✔ done  print(fit2) #> ── nlmixr² log-likelihood uobyqa ── #>  #>           OBJF      AIC     BIC Log-likelihood Condition#(Cov) Condition#(Cor) #> lPop -754.8202 1089.057 1103.78      -541.5284        99.51714        34.24803 #>  #> ── Time (sec $time): ── #>  #>              setup    optimize covariance preprocess postprocess table compress #> elapsed 0.05501568 0.005889803  8.424e-06      0.037       0.015 0.021        0 #>             other #> elapsed 0.5290861 #>  #> ── ($parFixed or $parFixedDf): ── #>  #>       Est.     SE  %RSE  Back-transformed(95%CI) BSV(SD) Shrink(SD)% #> E0  -1.041 0.2894  27.8 -1.041 (-1.608, -0.4738)                     #> Em   4.716  1.077 22.84     4.716 (2.605, 6.827)                     #> E50  1.827 0.5591  30.6    1.827 (0.7312, 2.923)                     #> g        2  FIXED FIXED                        2                     #>   #>   Covariance Type ($covMethod): r #>   Censoring ($censInformation): No censoring #>  #> ── Fit Data (object is a modified tibble): ── #> # A tibble: 1,000 × 5 #>   ID      TIME    DV  IPRED     v #>   <fct>  <dbl> <dbl>  <dbl> <dbl> #> 1 1     0.0490     0 -0.303 -1.04 #> 2 1     0.0520     0 -0.303 -1.04 #> 3 1     0.0525     0 -0.303 -1.04 #> # ℹ 997 more rows  # you can also get the nlm output with fit2$nlm  fit2$uobyqa #> parameter estimates: -1.04100744730652, 4.71613741001156, 1.82687976426281  #> objective: 541.528427129743  #> number of function evaluations: 58   # The nlm control has been modified slightly to include # extra components and name the parameters # }

code: # \donttest{  one.cmt <- function() {  ini({    ## You may label each parameter with a comment    tka <- 0.45 # Log Ka    tcl <- log(c(0, 2.7, 100)) # Log Cl    ## This works with interactive models    ## You may also label the preceding line with label("label text")    tv <- 3.45; label("log V")    ## the label("Label name") works with all models    eta.ka ~ 0.6    eta.cl ~ 0.3    eta.v ~ 0.1    add.sd <- 0.7  })  model({    ka <- exp(tka + eta.ka)    cl <- exp(tcl + eta.cl)    v <- exp(tv + eta.v)    linCmt() ~ add(add.sd)  }) }  fit <- nlmixr(one.cmt, theo_sd, est="focei") #>   #>   #>   #>   #> ℹ parameter labels from comments are typically ignored in non-interactive mode #> ℹ Need to run with the source intact to parse comments #> covType="analytic": a linCmt() model is out of analytic-covariance scope; using the finite-difference covariance instead #> → Calculating residuals/tables #> ✔ done  head(vpcSim(fit, pred=TRUE)) #>   #>   #>   sim.id id time     ipred        sim  tad        depot  central nlmixrRowNums #> 1      1  1 0.00  0.000000  0.9523465 0.00 3.199920e+02   0.0000             2 #> 2      1  1 0.25 10.014117 10.1728468 0.25 4.551650e+01 269.7569             3 #> 3      1  1 0.57 11.193601 11.3917494 0.57 3.750135e+00 301.5294             4 #> 4      1  1 1.12 10.688752 10.2964383 1.12 5.137032e-02 287.9300             5 #> 5      1  1 2.02  9.714757 10.6697232 2.02 4.587956e-05 261.6928             6 #> 6      1  1 3.82  8.022043  8.4837783 3.82 3.659597e-11 216.0951             7 #>   rxLambda rxYj rxLow rxHi     pred #> 1        1    2     0    1 0.000000 #> 2        1    2     0    1 3.263242 #> 3        1    2     0    1 5.830571 #> 4        1    2     0    1 7.865492 #> 5        1    2     0    1 8.505717 #> 6        1    2     0    1 7.621315  # }

new iterPrintControl() bundles every, ncol, headerEvery, useColor, simple options Added focei, foce, foi, fo mixture support nlmixr2est Fix focei mixture models llik residual distributions erroring model exactly one mixture probability parameter Fix fit$mixList returning first mixture component parHistData Back-Transformed rows now show mixture probability parameters natural probability scale Hardened mixture-model (mix()) estimation: clearer errors est="nlme" invalid initial probabilities, warnings underflowing/collapsing mixture probabilities, fix SAEM omega-diagonal floor raised outside mixture fits Fix segfault nlmSetup first estimator call fresh R session pooled estimators Fix heap-buffer overflow wrong back-transform SAEM lambda (Box-Cox) residual-error models Fix heap-buffer overflow wrong back-transform SAEM lambda (Box-Cox) residual-error models Guard null pointer arithmetic inner.cpp Use OpenMP threading S matrix calculation Use OpenMP threading wile calculating NPDEs

title: nlmixr2est 6.0.0
text: CRAN release: 2026-05-31 focei, foce, fo, laplace, agq successfully made thread safe parallelized (single CPU). default tolerance relaxation difficult solve ODEs changed per individual instead entire population (breaking change, major release). allow precision majority subjects optimization process. Add predict(fit, level="ipred"), predict(fit, level="individual") predict(fit, level=1) predict individual fits (possibly new dataset). Change test files .rds files Drop magrittr %>% favor |>. Breaking change: Minimum R version increased 4.0 4.1.0. change required support native pipe operator |>. Users R < 4.1.0 need upgrade R install version nlmixr2est. Bug fixes deparsing nlmixr2 control objects nlm related pooled methods now run parallel (based ID) Tests optimized reduce redundant fits run parallel. nlm (related pooled optimizers: bobyqa, newuoa, uobyqa, n1qn1, lbfgsb3c, optim, nlminb) now support censoring behavior (M2/M3/M4) FOCEI SAEM. $censInformation field populated fits way FOCEI/SAEM. agqControl() laplaceControl() now rxUiDeparse() methods can saved better packages like nlmixr2save shinyMixR. Added new outerOpt; methods focei related methods (agq, laplace, foce, fo, foi): “uobyqa” “newuoa”. saem methods now respect bounds default internally adding appropriate transform applying back-transformation just returning. parameters mu-referenced, breaks mu-referencing. breaks mu-referencing warning issued. best practice still unbounded parameters mu-referencing. want ignore behavior may use control=list(boundedTransform=FALSE) saem control=saemControl(boundedTransform=FALSE) mu referencing covariate procedure made less fragile support mu referencing conjunction iov bounded parameter transformations. Add bench-marking capabilities small speed fixes focei/saem

Change IOV implementation (#596): Now shows estimates CV% sd without shrinkage calculation. Allow different forms iov estimation, controlled iovXform. Retains iov parameter(s) output data.frame. iov, $omega shows list variability conditioning variable(s). fit$iov show IOV deviations conditioning variables(s) exception id IOV models can used estimation methods inherits ETA values. title: nlmixr2est 4.1.0
text: CRAN release: 2025-08-29 Updated inferring estimation method control object. Requires control object class length one match estimation method. example foceiControl() assume estimation method related focei. Changed Rstudio completion evaluate (case gets turned data.frames) (See #568) Turned data completion items like $fitMergeInner Breaking change: Changed estimation method posthoc add tables calculate covariance default. now method ’s control, posthocControl(). previously default include interaction term (can turn posthocControl(interaction=TRUE)). Added foceControl(), foControl() foiControl() foce, fo foi methods, respectively. try convert related control structures correct control structure estimation method. Added iov support focei, foce, saem (#614) Added new estimation method agq uses adaptive Gauss-Hermite Quadrature fit nonlinear-mixed effect model. method, can choose number quadrature points estimate likelihood, higher numbers giving accurate likelihoods. AGQ implementation nlmixr2est allows specify number quadrature points via agqControl() function, supports single multiple subject models. method particularly useful models accurate likelihood estimation critical. Also added laplace method agq 1 node (numerically focei, foce log-likelihood focei/laplace, etc), uses agq routine. Fixed saem mu-reference display compressing internal item saem0.

Add mceta option "focei". mceta=-1 default; eta restarts best eta last step start inner optimization. mceta=0 eta starts 0 start inner optimization. mceta=1 eta starts either 0 best eta, ever gives lowest objective function start inner optimization. mceta=n assumption omega sample n-1 eta values use lowest objective function eta sampled, last best eta eta=0 start inner optimization. title: nlmixr2est 2.0.6 – new package
text: Remove lower level foceiFit function. Focei, foce, fo, foi, posthoc now directly takes rxode2 ui objects New error types supported focei including mixing theta etas residual errors different types proportional errors Different types additive proportional errors can used endpoint using + combined1() + combined2() otherwise takes supplied addProp option figure type combined model run (default combined2()) Focei model cache now named focei-md5Digest.qs uses qs compression/saving/loading. foceiControl() aligned methods. foceiControl(adjLik=TRUE) uses NONMEM-style objective function throughout. foceiControl(adjLik=FALSE) uses adjusted objective function throughout, adjusts back NONMEM objective function. Lag time subject variability differences longer calculate ideal relative step size, absolute step size using Gill differences (default) Objective function checks infinite/NaN/NA values entire solving space ensures overflow occurs calculating inner hessian

title: nlmixr2est 2.0.6 – new package
text: mu referencing longer required saem; Internally non mu-referenced values converted mu referenced values converted back calculating nlmixr2 object. nlmixr2 forced parameter ordering (1) population effects, non mu-referenced subject effects (3) omega estimates (4) residual effects. changes order nlmixr2 sees parameters. Since based random number generator, optimization trajectory different different results nlmixr Components omega can now fixed. Residual error components can also fixed. optimizing one residual value, nlmixr2’s saem uses nlm R, efficient nealder-meade method. Lower level saem functions (like configsaem()) exported increasingly difficult use convert something standard; methods (like print, summary etc) maintained view lower level object debugging . Parameter history print-longer includes fixed parameters. model calculate residuals closely matches model used estimation remove small rounding differences may occur models. Different types additive proportional errors can used endpoint using + combined1() + combined2() otherwise takes supplied addProp option figure type combined model run (default combined2()) Parameter history printout now uses standard deviation additive components, matching estimation components. rxode2 solving options now saved rxControl part saemControl(). saemControl(rxControl=rxControl(...)); fixes conflicting option names well allowing alignment control structures focei, nlme saem saemControl() aligned methods.

title: nlmixr2est 2.0.6 – new package

Also aligned methods. weights, fixed, random can specified nlmixr2NlmeControl(). , nlme object returned. returnNlme new option return nlme object instead traditional nlme object. nlme_ode lme_lin_cmpt removed. rxode2 solving options now saved rxControl part saemControl(). nlmeControl(rxControl=rxControl(...)); fixes conflicting option names well allowing alignment control structures focei, nlme saem
